demographics.living_situation: resident in an aged care facility, fully dependent for care

contact_nok.relationship: aged care nursing staff (present and calling on the resident's behalf)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: They won't stop vomiting, they are extremely confused, and their breath smells strongly of urine. - Nursing Staff Report
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Nursing staff at the aged care facility are calling after hours because over the last half-day the resident has become badly confused, keeps vomiting, and is very swollen and short of breath.

symptom_narrative.onset: gradual over about 48 hours, with the confusion coming on in the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: two days of worsening, confusion in the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.character: extreme tiredness and flatness, non-stop nausea and vomiting, not eating, very swollen legs, itching all over, breathless on any effort, and now muddled and disoriented
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: nursing staff say the resident is the worst they have seen them — bedbound, drowsy and not making sense
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: swelling in the legs and body; sickness in the stomach; confusion in the head
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: steadily worse over two days, then a sharp change with confusion in the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — the usual fluid tablets have stopped making any urine and the anti-sickness medicine hasn't helped
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it keeps getting worse on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a strong urine-like smell on the breath, intense itching with scratch marks, losing weight over the past month, and vomiting up bilious fluid
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: known long-standing kidney failure managed at the facility
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely bedbound and dependent, too drowsy and confused to look after themselves

past_medical_history.how_managed: managed at the aged care facility, on fluid tablets

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in an aged care facility, fully dependent on nursing staff; staff present and making the call

objective_data_offered.value: 195/110 on the facility blood pressure cuff
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading taken by nursing staff on a facility cuff

objective_data_offered.value: breathing fast, around 24 breaths a minute
oxygen level 92% on the facility finger monitor
looks pale with waxy yellowish skin, very swollen weeping legs, confused and disoriented, scratching at the arms, vomiting bilious fluid into emesis bags
a strong urine-like smell on the breath noticed by staff; the water tablets are producing no urine at all
